prompt_text: Apify vs Scrapy: which is better?
response:
Apify is **better if you want a managed, cloud-based platform with minimal DevOps and lots of no‑code/low‑code options**, while Scrapy is **better if you want a free, highly customizable Python framework you control end‑to‑end**.[1][6]

Here’s a focused comparison to help you choose.

| Aspect | **Apify** | **Scrapy** |
|-------|-----------|------------|
| What it is | Cloud **platform** for web scraping & automation (Actors, UI, storage, scheduling) | Open‑source **Python framework** for building spiders |
| Ease of use | Easier for non‑developers; UI, templates, marketplace of prebuilt scrapers[1][3] | Requires solid Python skills and CLI familiarity[1][5] |
| Hosting & scaling | Built‑in cloud execution, autoscaling, scheduling, storage, proxy integration[1][2] | You must handle servers, scheduling (e.g., cron), deployment, proxies yourself[1] |
| Customization | High, but within Apify’s platform model; supports any language via Actors[2] | Very high: full control over spiders, middlewares, pipelines, code[1][5] |
| Cost | SaaS pricing; free tier + paid plans, can get expensive at scale[1][4] | Framework is free/open‑source; you only pay for your own infra/proxies[1][4] |
| Best for | Teams needing **managed infrastructure**, fast setup, marketplace, no‑code flows[1][3][6] | Developers needing **maximum control**, complex custom logic, no vendor lock‑in[1][5][6] |

### When Apify is “better”

Choose **Apify** if you:

- Want **managed cloud infrastructure**: job scheduling, autoscaling, storage, logs, queueing, proxies, etc., handled for you.[1][2]  
- Prefer **no‑code or low‑code** tools, visual workflows, and a **marketplace** of ready‑made scrapers/automations.[1][3]  
- Need to **deploy scrapers quickly** without building deployment and monitoring from scratch.[2][6]  
- Work with a mixed team (non‑technical + developers) and want a shared UI and API-based platform.[1][3]

Apify is effectively a **full web scraping platform**, not just a library, so it reduces DevOps and productization work significantly.[2][6]

### When Scrapy is “better”

Choose **Scrapy** if you:

- Are comfortable with **Python** and want a **framework** you can fully customize.[1][5]  
- Need fine‑grained control over crawling logic, middlewares, throttling, pipelines, and integration with your own stack.[1][5]  
- Prefer **open‑source** (no vendor lock‑in) and want to host everything on your own infrastructure.[1][4]  
- Are building **large, complex crawlers** as part of an engineering codebase and already have CI/CD, servers, and monitoring.[1][5][6]

Scrapy excels as a developer tool with maximum flexibility, but you must build/maintain the surrounding infrastructure yourself.[1]

### Practical rule of thumb

- If your main pain is: **“I don’t want to manage servers, proxies, schedulers, and a UI; I just want results” → Apify**.  
- If your main pain is: **“I need deep customization in Python and control over everything, and I’m fine managing infra” → Scrapy**.

If you tell me:
- your tech stack,
- team’s Python level,
- whether you can manage your own servers,
- approximate scale (pages/day),
I can give a concrete recommendation for your specific case.
